title: Neoregelia concentrica
taxonomy: Neoregelia concentrica is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a type of bromeliad, which is a group of tropical plants characterized by their distinctive leaves and ability to form rosettes. Bromeliads are further divided into several genera, with Neoregelia being one of the most common and widely cultivated. Neoregelia concentrica is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in the understory of rainforests. This plant is often grown as an ornamental for its vibrant flowers and attractive foliage.

Some studies suggest that the plant's compounds may have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which could contribute to its medicinal potential. However, further investigation is necessary to fully understand its benefits and potential side effects. More research is required to confirm the specific medicinal uses of Neoregelia concentrica.
